sources.title: Is GameStop Corp. (GME) A Good Stock To Buy Now?
sources.excerpt: **Is GME a good stock to buy?** We came across a [bullish thesis ](https://www.valuedontlie.com/p/quick-value-309-gamestop-gme)on GameStop Corp. on Value Don't Lie's Substack. In this article, we will summarize the bulls' thesis on GME. GameStop Corp.'s share was trading at $22.26 as of June 8th. GME's trailing P/E was 16.27 according to Yahoo Finance. Caesars Entertainment (CZR): JPMorgan Highlights Digital Segment for Earnings Potential Amid Gaming Sector Negativity magicinfoto / Shutterstock.com GameStop Corp., a specialty retailer, provides games, collectibles, and entertainment products through its stores and e-commerce platforms in United States and internationally. GME has emerged from the meme-stock era as a dramatically different company under CEO Ryan Cohen, with the business now approaching its first profitable year since 2018 while sitting on more than $9 billion of cash and securities that could fund a transformational acquisition. **Read More:**[**15 AI Stocks That Are Quietly Making Investors Rich**](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/15-ai-stocks-that-are-quietly- making-investors-rich-1714967/) **Read More:** [**Undervalued AI Stock Poised For Massive Gains: 10000% Upside Potential**](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/undervalued-ai-stock-poised- for-massive-gains-10000-upside-19/) Since becoming Chairman in 2021 and CEO in late 2023, Cohen has aggressively streamlined operations by accelerating store closures, reducing SG&A expenses, improving gross margins through a higher mix of collectibles, and capitalizing on a favorable lease structure that allowed the company to exit underperforming locations quickly. GameStop's store base has declined from more than 7,000 locations at its peak to roughly 3,200 stores globally, but profitability has improved su

analysis:
- **External commentary:** [1] notes GameStop trades around $22 with an “indicated intrinsic discount of about 69%” and a fair value of $220 per share under the most popular narrative, though it cautions that recent returns are choppy (down 3.2% one month, down 8.2% three months). [2] highlights a bullish thesis citing CEO Ryan Cohen’s operational turnaround: store count reduced from 7,000+ to ~3,200, improved gross margins via collectibles mix, and over $9 billion in cash/securities that could fund a transformational acquisition. The source also notes GME is approaching its first profitable year since 2018.

- **Why it can work:** The company holds a large cash position (~$9B) relative to its ~$9.7B market cap, providing a potential floor and optionality for acquisitions or share buybacks. Operational improvements under Cohen (cost cuts, store rationalization, margin expansion) are supported by [2] and could lead to sustained profitability. The low forward P/E of 18.4 and PEG of 0.31 suggest the market is not pricing in future earnings growth, leaving room for upside if execution continues.

- **What can break it:** The core retail business (video games, collectibles) faces secular decline as digital downloads replace physical media; store count has already shrunk dramatically. The high cash balance may be a “value trap” if management fails to deploy it accretively. Short interest at 13.1% of float indicates persistent bearish sentiment, and the stock’s 1-month return (-0.6%) lags the sector median (+6.6%), showing no near-term catalyst. The RSI acceleration of -15.3 suggests momentum is deteriorating, not improving.

- **Confirming evidence to watch:** Look for concrete M&A announcements or share repurchase programs using the $9B cash pile. Monitor quarterly earnings for continued gross margin expansion and positive net income (first profitable year since 2018 per [2]). A sustained increase in revenue from collectibles or e-commerce would signal the business model is stabilizing. Any insider buying by Cohen or other executives would be a strong signal.

- **Bottom line:** GameStop presents a high-conviction turnaround narrative with a cash-rich balance sheet and operational improvements, but the stock’s recent price action and sector lag suggest the market remains skeptical. The long setup depends entirely on management’s ability to deploy cash and sustain profitability—unknowns that are not yet confirmed by the data. No trade recommendation.

sources.title: SBRA vs. CUBE: Which Stock Is the Better Value Option?
sources.excerpt: Investors looking for stocks in the REIT and Equity Trust - Other sector might want to consider either Sabra Healthcare (SBRA) or CubeSmart (CUBE). But which of these two stocks offers value investors a better bang for their buck right now? We'll need to take a closer look. Everyone has their own methods for finding great value opportunities, but our model includes pairing an impressive grade in the Value category of our Style Scores system with a strong Zacks Rank. The proven Zacks Rank emphasizes companies with positive estimate revision trends, and our Style Scores highlight stocks with specific traits. Sabra Healthcare has a Zacks Rank of #2 (Buy), while CubeSmart has a Zacks Rank of #3 (Hold) right now. Investors should feel comfortable knowing that SBRA likely has seen a stronger improvement to its earnings outlook than CUBE has recently. But this is only part of the picture for value investors. Value investors also tend to look at a number of traditional, tried-and-true figures to help them find stocks that they believe are undervalued at their current share price levels. Our Value category grades stocks based on a number of key metrics, including the tried-and-true P/E ratio, the P/S ratio, earnings yield, and cash flow per share, as well as a variety of other fundamentals that value investors frequently use. SBRA currently has a forward P/E ratio of 11.91, while CUBE has a forward P/E of 15.61. We also note that SBRA has a PEG ratio of 1.35. This metric is used similarly to the famous P/E ratio, but the PEG ratio also takes into account the stock's expected earnings growth rate. CUBE currently has a PEG ratio of 6.95. Another notable valuation metric for SBRA is its P/B ratio of 1.67. The P/B ratio is used to compare a stock's market value with its book value,

sources.title: FVR vs. SBRA: Which Stock Is the Better Value Option?
sources.excerpt: Investors with an interest in REIT and Equity Trust - Other stocks have likely encountered both FrontView REIT, Inc. (FVR) and Sabra Healthcare (SBRA). But which of these two stocks is more attractive to value investors? We'll need to take a closer look to find out. There are plenty of strategies for discovering value stocks, but we have found that pairing a strong Zacks Rank with an impressive grade in the Value category of our Style Scores system produces the best returns. The Zacks Rank favors stocks with strong earnings estimate revision trends, and our Style Scores highlight companies with specific traits. Right now, FrontView REIT, Inc. is sporting a Zacks Rank of #2 (Buy), while Sabra Healthcare has a Zacks Rank of #3 (Hold). The Zacks Rank favors stocks that have recently seen positive revisions to their earnings estimates, so investors should rest assured that FVR has an improving earnings outlook. But this is just one piece of the puzzle for value investors. Value investors also tend to look at a number of traditional, tried-and-true figures to help them find stocks that they believe are undervalued at their current share price levels. Our Value category highlights undervalued companies by looking at a variety of key metrics, including the popular P/E ratio, as well as the P/S ratio, earnings yield, cash flow per share, and a variety of other fundamentals that have been used by value investors for years. FVR currently has a forward P/E ratio of 13.38, while SBRA has a forward P/E of 13.57. We also note that FVR has a PEG ratio of 0.79. This popular figure is similar to the widely-used P/E ratio, but the PEG ratio also considers a company's expected EPS growth rate. SBRA currently has a PEG ratio of 1.63. Another notable valuation metric for FVR is its P/B rati

analysis:
- **External commentary:** Source [1] notes SBRA has a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy) and a forward P/E of 11.91, which is lower than peer CUBE’s 15.61, and a PEG ratio of 1.35 versus CUBE’s 6.95, suggesting relative value. However, source [3] contradicts this, assigning SBRA a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold) and a higher forward P/E of 13.57 versus FVR’s 13.38, with a higher PEG of 1.63 versus FVR’s 0.79. Source [2] reports no recent analyst price target changes, indicating a neutral near-term catalyst outlook.

- **Why it can work:** The warehouse data shows a high opportunity score (93.4) and a squeeze/laggard score (93.4), combined with elevated short interest (11.6% of float) and a 1-month return of -10.2% versus sector median +4.0%, implying potential for mean reversion or short squeeze. Source [1] supports a value thesis with lower forward P/E and PEG versus one peer, suggesting possible undervaluation if earnings estimates hold.

- **What can break it:** The stock is a 1-month peer laggard (-14.2% vs. sector), with negative RSI momentum (RSI 42.2, prior 3-bar avg delta -5.6) and a high forward P/E of 24.43 (per warehouse data), which contradicts the lower 11.91 figure in source [1]—unknown which is accurate. Source [3] shows a weaker Zacks Rank (Hold) versus another peer, and source [2] indicates no bullish catalyst from analysts. High PEG (5.28 per warehouse) suggests expensive growth expectations.

- **Confirming evidence to watch:** Look for a sustained RSI acceleration above 50 and positive RSI delta over the next 1-2 weeks. Monitor short interest changes and any earnings estimate revisions upward, as source [1] ties Zacks Rank to positive revisions. A sector-relative price breakout above the 1-month peer lag would signal reversal. Unknown if upcoming earnings or REIT operational metrics (e.g., occupancy, rent collections) support the value case.

- **Bottom line:** SBRA shows a mixed setup: high short interest and lagging price action create squeeze potential, and source [1] suggests relative value versus one peer. However, conflicting valuation data (forward P/E ranges from 11.91 to 24.43), a Hold rank in source [3], and no analyst catalyst per source [2] introduce significant uncertainty. The long thesis depends on mean reversion and short covering, not confirmed fundamentals. No trade recommendation.

sources.title: Is Albertsons Companies (ACI) Stock Undervalued Right Now?
sources.excerpt: Here at Zacks, we focus on our proven ranking system, which places an emphasis on earnings estimates and estimate revisions, to find winning stocks. But we also understand that investors develop their own strategies, so we are constantly looking at the latest trends in value, growth, and momentum to find strong companies for our readers. Of these, perhaps no stock market trend is more popular than value investing, which is a strategy that has proven to be successful in all sorts of market environments. Value investors use a variety of methods, including tried-and- true valuation metrics, to find these stocks. In addition to the Zacks Rank, investors looking for stocks with specific traits can utilize our Style Scores system. Of course, value investors will be most interested in the system's "Value" category. Stocks with "A" grades for Value and high Zacks Ranks are among the best value stocks available at any given moment. One stock to keep an eye on is Albertsons Companies (ACI). ACI is currently sporting a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy), as well as a Value grade of A. The stock has a Forward P/E ratio of 8.29. This compares to its industry's average Forward P/E of 18.19. ACI's Forward P/E has been as high as 10.44 and as low as 7.46, with a median of 8.91, all within the past year. We also note that ACI holds a PEG ratio of 1.66. This popular figure is similar to the widely-used P/E ratio, but the PEG ratio also considers a company's expected EPS growth rate. ACI's industry has an average PEG of 2.69 right now. Over the last 12 months, ACI's PEG has been as high as 2.09 and as low as 0.93, with a median of 1.78. Investors should also recognize that ACI has a P/B ratio of 3.11. Investors use the P/B ratio to look at a stock's market value versus its book value, which is defined a

analysis:
- **External commentary:** Zacks Rank #2 (Buy) and a Value grade of A are cited, with a forward P/E of 6.58–8.29 versus industry average 18.19, and a PEG of 1.59–1.66 versus industry 2.69 [1][2]. The source explicitly compares ACI favorably to L’Oreal on valuation metrics and earnings estimate revisions [1]. No commentary addresses the recent price decline, short interest, or operational risks.

- **Why it can work:** The stock trades at a deep discount to its staples peer group on forward P/E (6.6x vs. industry ~18x) and PEG (1.6x vs. 2.7x), which could attract value-oriented capital if earnings estimates hold [1][2]. The Zacks Rank #2 suggests positive estimate revision momentum, a typical catalyst for mean reversion in beaten-down names.

- **What can break it:** The trailing P/E of 40.35 implies past earnings were depressed—possibly due to one-time charges or margin compression—making the forward multiple less reliable if those charges recur. Short interest at 9.3% of float and a 1-month return of -8.0% (vs. sector median +5.4%) indicate persistent selling pressure. The RSI acceleration of -15.4 suggests momentum is still deteriorating, not stabilizing. Unknown: whether the low forward P/E reflects structural margin erosion (e.g., from wage inflation, shrink, or competition from discounters) or a temporary earnings dip.

- **Confirming evidence to watch:** Stabilization or reversal of the RSI acceleration (i.e., RSI delta turning less negative), a reduction in short interest, or positive same-store sales / margin guidance in the next earnings report. Also watch for insider buying or an increase in estimate revisions breadth beyond the Zacks Rank signal.

- **Bottom line:** ACI screens as statistically cheap on forward multiples with a favorable analyst rank, but the price action and momentum are strongly negative. The long setup depends on whether the low valuation is a genuine discount or a value trap driven by unresolved operational headwinds. No trade recommendation.

sources.excerpt: * In recent weeks, The Trade Desk resolved its high-profile billing dispute with Publicis, appointed Nate Olmstead as CFO, added veteran executive David Haddad to its board, and hired former Zendesk leader Sarah Gavin as CMO and Executive Vice President. * These moves collectively reduce a key agency relationship risk while bolstering financial, marketing, and governance leadership at a moment when the company's role in the digital ad ecosystem is under close scrutiny. * Now we'll examine how resolving the Publicis dispute may reshape The Trade Desk's investment narrative and investors' perception of its platform. Capitalize on the AI infrastructure supercycle with our selection of the [48 best 'picks and shovels' of the AI gold rush](https://simplywall.st/discover/investing-ideas/473744/ai-infrastructure- stocks/global?utm_medium=finance_user&utm_campaign=intro&utm_source=yahoo&blueprint=4626176) converting record-breaking demand into massive cash flow. ## Trade Desk Investment Narrative Recap The core Trade Desk thesis still rests on the open internet gaining share in data driven, measurable advertising, particularly in connected TV. Resolving the Publicis billing dispute directly addresses the biggest near term risk around agency concentration, while the key short term catalyst remains whether major brands actually sustain or increase spend on the platform after this resolution. Among the new announcements, the settlement with Publicis is most relevant, as it restores the agency's recommendation of Trade Desk's platform to clients at a time when stock-specific concerns, technical pressure, and questions about long term growth have weighed heavily on sentiment and valuation. Yet while the Publicis dispute is resolved, investors should be aware that agency audits and f

sources.title: Can TTD Capitalize on Agoda's High-Intent Travel Audience Across APAC?
sources.excerpt: The digital advertising landscape in the Asia-Pacific (APAC) region is evolving rapidly, driven by the convergence of first-party data, programmatic advertising and changing consumer travel behaviors. Against this backdrop, the alliance between **The Trade Desk, Inc.** TTD and Agoda presents a compelling opportunity to transform travel advertising across the region. The collaboration allows advertisers to leverage Agoda's rich first-party travel data through Agoda Media Solutions, part of **Booking Holdings Inc.** BKNG advertising network, enabling marketers to target high-intent travelers more effectively across premium digital channels. Through this initiative, Agoda becomes The Trade Desk's first online travel agency partner in APAC to extend audience targeting beyond its own platform. By integrating with TTD's advertising platform, brands gain access to audience segments built from Agoda's first-party travel data, allowing them to engage consumers throughout the travel planning process. The combined efforts now allow advertisers to reach travelers much earlier in their journey. Brands can connect with audiences across various digital platforms, including CTV, OTT streaming services, streaming music platforms, podcasts, display ads, mobile apps and digital out-of-home advertising. Agoda's platform collects significant amounts of first-party travel data from users actively researching and planning trips. This information provides advertisers with insights into travel interests, destination preferences, booking behaviors, seasonal travel patterns, geographic trends and consumer engagement signals. When paired with TTD's advanced audience targeting and media buying tools, advertisers can create highly targeted campaigns that reach consumers with remarkable relevance. Th

sources.title: Is The Trade Desk, Inc. (TTD) A Good Stock To Buy Now?
sources.excerpt: **Is TTD a good stock to buy?** We came across a [bullish thesis ](https://www.reddit.com/r/ValueInvesting/comments/1rxmshz/ttd_still_looks_dislocated_even_after_the/)on The Trade Desk, Inc. on r/ValueInvesting by NathanGuoKL. In this article, we will summarize the bulls' thesis on TTD. The Trade Desk, Inc.'s share was trading at $19.43 as of June 8th. TTD's trailing and forward P/E were 23.90 and 18.94 respectively according to Yahoo Finance. Needham Keeps Buy Rating on Klaviyo (KVYO), Highlights Strong Core Metrics, Successful Segment Execution The Trade Desk, Inc. operates as a technology company in the United States and internationally. TTD is currently trading at $23.55, compared to an estimated intrinsic value of $49.26 based on FCFF revenue assumptions, implying substantial upside and a market-implied free cash flow growth rate of just 0.92%, which appears overly pessimistic. The market is increasingly pricing in a negative narrative driven by the Publicis dispute, assuming it signals a broader agency revolt, margin compression, and a structural break in programmatic advertising and CTV growth. **Read More:**[**15 AI Stocks That Are Quietly Making Investors Rich**](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/15-ai-stocks-that-are-quietly- making-investors-rich-1714967/) **Read More:** [**Undervalued AI Stock Poised For Massive Gains: 10000% Upside Potential**](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/undervalued-ai-stock-poised- for-massive-gains-10000-upside-19/) However, this interpretation appears excessive relative to the underlying fundamentals. Publicis does represent a near-term risk, as TTD disclosed that two holding companies each account for more than 10% of gross billings and together represent roughly 30%, with Publicis believed to be one of them, creating a potenti

analysis:
- **External commentary:** [1] notes the Publicis billing dispute resolution, new CFO, and board additions reduce agency relationship risk but warns that “the key short term catalyst remains whether major brands actually sustain or increase spend.” [2] highlights a new APAC partnership with Agoda for first-party travel data targeting across CTV and digital channels. [3] cites a bullish thesis that TTD’s intrinsic value is ~$49 based on FCFF, with the market pricing in an overly negative narrative around the Publicis dispute, though it acknowledges Publicis represents ~30% of gross billings.

- **Why it can work:** The Publicis truce removes a major overhang that had depressed sentiment and valuation, potentially restoring agency recommendations and client spend. The Agoda partnership opens a new revenue stream in high-growth APAC travel advertising, leveraging first-party data for CTV and programmatic targeting. At a forward P/E of 9.04 and PEG of 0.95, the stock appears cheap relative to historical multiples if growth stabilizes, and the RSI acceleration from -1.6 to +7.6 suggests short-term momentum improvement.

- **What can break it:** The Publicis dispute resolution may not translate into sustained spend increases if brands remain cautious or shift budgets to competitors. Agency concentration risk persists—two holding companies account for ~30% of gross billings [3]. The Agoda partnership is nascent and unproven in revenue contribution. Short interest at 16.3% of float indicates persistent bearish conviction, and the stock is down 79.3% from its 52-week high, suggesting structural concerns beyond the Publicis issue. Sentiment score of -0.6 remains negative.

- **Confirming evidence to watch:** Track quarterly revenue growth and forward guidance for acceleration, especially from CTV and international segments. Monitor agency partner commentary (e.g., Publicis earnings calls) for explicit recommitment to TTD’s platform. Look for increased advertiser adoption of the Agoda data integration in APAC. Watch for short interest declines below 10% and sustained RSI above 50. Any insider buying or analyst upgrades would be supportive.

- **Bottom line:** The setup is a contrarian value play with a catalyst from the Publicis resolution and a new growth angle in APAC travel, but the stock’s deep drawdown and high short interest reflect unresolved skepticism about agency concentration and growth sustainability. The low forward P/E and PEG suggest limited downside if fundamentals stabilize, but confirmation of revenue reacceleration is required before the thesis is validated. No trade recommendation.

sources.title: Is CHWY Stock a Buy After Its Selloff and Lower Revenue View
sources.excerpt: **Chewy, Inc.** CHWY has become harder to frame after a sharp selloff and lower fiscal 2026 revenue outlook. The question is whether the decline has created a better entry point or whether softer consumer behavior still argues for patience. Investors need to weigh valuation against recurring revenue strength, margin progress and execution risk. ## Why CHWY Looks Cheaper on Sales Multiples CHWY, which carries a Zacks Rank #4 (Sell), trades at 0.56X forward 12-month sales. That is well below the Zacks sub-industry at 1.99X, the Zacks sector at 1.50X and the S&P 500 at 5.17X. You can see **the complete list of today's Zacks #1 Rank (Strong Buy) stocks here**. The stock is also near the low end of its five-year sales multiple range. CHWY has traded as high as 4.01X, as low as 0.55X and at a median of 1.18X. That helps explain why some investors may see less downside from valuation compression after the stock's 42.9% year-to-date decline and 54.6% slide over the past year. ## Why Chewy Still Supports a Bullish Case Chewy's bull case still starts with Autoship, which gives the business a recurring revenue base that many retailers lack. In the first quarter of fiscal 2026, Autoship customer sales rose 10.5% year over year to $2.83 billion and represented 84.4% of net sales. Customer metrics remain supportive. Active customers increased 3.6% year over year to 21.5 million, while net sales per active customer reached $597. Pet health, SmartPak and Modern Animal add another layer by expanding Chewy beyond core online retail and into higher-engagement services. Profitability also keeps the debate from turning one-sided. First-quarter adjusted EBITDA rose 31.3% to $253.1 million, and adjusted EBITDA margin expanded 130 basis points to 7.5%. Fiscal 2026 adjusted EBITDA margin guidan

sources.title: CHWY's Push Into Veterinary Services Broadens Its Addressable Market
sources.excerpt: **Chewy, Inc.** CHWY is increasingly leveraging its veterinary care operations as a powerful tool for customer acquisition and retention. Management highlighted that Chewy Vet Care ("CVC") clinics are delivering strong stand- alone economics, while strengthening the broader Chewy ecosystem. The company views pet healthcare as a major growth opportunity, with an estimated total addressable market of approximately $54 billion, including more than $40 billion tied to veterinary services and in-clinic products. The company's vet care strategy is already generating meaningful customer growth. According to management, roughly 40% of CVC customers are new to Chewy. These customers tend to become highly valuable over time, reaching approximately $900 in first-year net sales per active customer. In addition, existing Chewy customers who visit CVC clinics increase their share of wallet with the company at a faster rate than other customer groups, highlighting the effectiveness of the clinics in driving deeper customer engagement. Chewy believes its technology-enabled operating model provides an advantage in veterinary services. The company utilizes AI-assisted tools and digital workflows to improve clinic productivity, veterinarian retention and employee satisfaction. Management noted that these capabilities are particularly important in an environment where qualified veterinarians remain in short supply, giving Chewy a structural advantage as it expands its clinic network. To accelerate growth, Chewy recently completed the acquisition of Modern Animal, a technology-focused veterinary clinic operator. The transaction adds an established clinic footprint, strong clinical expertise and attractive unit economics that complement the Chewy Vet Care model. Following the acquisition, th

sources.title: Is Chewy (CHWY) One of the Most Promising Mid-Cap Stocks to Buy According to Analysts?
sources.excerpt: Chewy Inc. (NYSE:[**CHWY**](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/CHWY)) is one of the [**most promising mid-cap stocks to buy according to analysts**](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/10-most-promising-mid-cap- stocks-to-buy-according-to-analysts-1778668/). On June 10, Chewy reported its FQ1 2026 financial results, highlighting continued growth in revenue and profitability. The company posted net sales of $3.36 billion, representing a 7.7% year-over-year increase, alongside improved gross margin and net income performance. Profitability metrics strengthened during the quarter, with net income reaching $94.8 million and adjusted EBITDA rising to $253.1 million. Margins also expanded across multiple measures, reflecting improved operational efficiency and continued scaling of the company's digital retail platform. Management attributed the results to strong customer growth, market share gains in the pet category, and disciplined execution across its business model. The company emphasized its ongoing focus on profitable expansion, customer acquisition, and long-term shareholder value creation in a competitive consumer environment. Is Chewy (CHWY) One of the Most Promising Mid-Cap Stocks to Buy According to Analysts? Copyright: [antonioguillem / 123RF Stock Photo](http://www.123rf.com/profile_antonioguillem) Chewy Inc. (NYSE:CHWY) is involved in the e-commerce business across the United States. The company provides pet supplies and medications, pet food and treats, and other pet health products. It is based in Plantation, Florida, and was incorporated in 2010. While we acknowledge the potential of CHWY as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. If you're looking for an extremely undervalued AI stock that also sta

analysis:
- **External commentary**: Sources highlight Chewy’s deeply discounted sales multiple (0.56x forward sales vs. sub-industry 1.99x) and near five-year lows [1]. Analysts note Autoship recurring revenue (84.4% of sales, +10.5% YoY) and expanding adjusted EBITDA margins (+130 bps to 7.5%) [1]. Veterinary services are cited as a $54B TAM driver, with 40% of CVC customers new to Chewy and first-year spend ~$900 [2]. The stock is labeled a “promising mid-cap” by analysts, with FQ1 net sales +7.7% YoY and net income of $94.8M [3].

- **Why it can work**: The low sales multiple (0.56x) already prices in significant pessimism, limiting further valuation compression if fundamentals stabilize. Autoship provides a sticky, subscription-like revenue base that supports cash flow predictability. The vet care expansion (CVC + Modern Animal) opens a large adjacent market and drives higher customer lifetime value, as evidenced by increased share of wallet from existing customers [2]. Margin expansion (EBITDA +31% YoY) shows operating leverage is improving despite slower top-line growth.

- **What can break it**: The lowered fiscal 2026 revenue outlook signals softer consumer demand, which could persist if pet spending weakens further [1]. The stock is down 54.6% over the past year and 42.9% YTD, indicating negative momentum that may not reverse quickly. Short interest at 6.8% is moderate but not extreme enough to force a squeeze. The trailing P/E of 38.75x is elevated relative to forward P/E of 10.3x, suggesting earnings expectations have dropped sharply—any further cuts would pressure the stock. Execution risk in scaling vet clinics (capital intensity, veterinarian shortages) is unknown from excerpts.

- **Confirming evidence to watch**: Monitor quarterly Autoship sales growth (must stay above 8-10% YoY) and active customer trends (current +3.6% YoY). Watch for stabilization or upward revision in fiscal 2026 revenue guidance. Track CVC clinic unit economics and customer acquisition costs—if 40% new customer rate persists, it validates the vet strategy. Also watch adjusted EBITDA margin trajectory; current 7.5% needs to expand toward double digits to justify the forward P/E.

- **Bottom line**: Chewy presents a contrarian long setup based on a deeply discounted sales multiple, recurring Autoship revenue, and a vet-care expansion that could re-rate the stock if consumer sentiment improves. However, the lowered revenue outlook and negative price momentum are real headwinds. The setup is speculative and depends on stabilization of top-line growth and continued margin expansion—neither is guaranteed. No trade recommendation.

sources.title: How Much Upside is Left in UiPath (PATH)? Wall Street Analysts Think 25.21%
sources.excerpt: Shares of **UiPath** (PATH) have gained 2.7% over the past four weeks to close the last trading session at $10.55, but there could still be a solid upside left in the stock if short-term price targets of Wall Street analysts are any indication. Going by the price targets, the mean estimate of $13.21 indicates a potential upside of 25.2%. The mean estimate comprises 14 short-term price targets with a standard deviation of $1.12. While the lowest estimate of $12.00 indicates a 13.7% increase from the current price level, the most optimistic analyst expects the stock to surge 42.2% to reach $15.00. It's very important to note the standard deviation here, as it helps understand the variability of the estimates. The smaller the standard deviation, the greater the agreement among analysts. While the consensus price target is a much-coveted metric for investors, solely banking on this metric to make an investment decision may not be wise at all. That's because the ability and unbiasedness of analysts in setting price targets have long been questionable. However, an impressive consensus price target is not the only factor that indicates a potential upside in PATH. This view is strengthened by the agreement among analysts that the company will report better earnings than what they estimated earlier. Though a positive trend in earnings estimate revisions doesn't give any idea as to how much the stock could surge, it has proven effective in predicting an upside. ## Price, Consensus and EPS Surprise Zacks Price, Consensus and EPS Surprise Chart for PATH ## Here's What You Should Know About Analysts' Price Targets According to researchers at several universities across the globe, a price target is one of many pieces of information about a stock that misleads investors far more often

sources.title: Is UiPath, Inc. (PATH) A Good Stock To Buy Now?
sources.excerpt: **Is PATH a good stock to buy?** We came across a [bullish thesis ](https://montanamatos.substack.com/p/uipath-agentic-ai-investment-thesis)on UiPath, Inc. on Montana Insights's Substack by Montana Matos. Here is why Aptiv (APTV) is Among the 7 Best Strong Buy European Stocks to Invest In Copyright: [microolga / 123RF Stock Photo](http://www.123rf.com/profile_microolga) UiPath, Inc. provides an automation platform that offers a range of robotic process automation (RPA) solutions primarily in the United States and internationally. PATH is being mispriced by the market as a legacy robotic process automation company facing obsolescence from generative AI, while the bullish thesis argues the company is actually becoming critical infrastructure for the emerging agentic AI era. The market assumes large language models will replace UiPath's software, but enterprises still require controlled execution, governance, compliance, auditability, and workflow orchestration to deploy AI safely in production environments. **Read More:**[**15 AI Stocks That Are Quietly Making Investors Rich**](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/15-ai-stocks-that-are-quietly- making-investors-rich-1714967/) **Read More:** [**Undervalued AI Stock Poised For Massive Gains: 10000% Upside Potential**](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/undervalued-ai-stock-poised- for-massive-gains-10000-upside-19/) UiPath's Maestro platform positions the company as the orchestration layer coordinating humans, bots, AI agents, and enterprise systems, allowing it to benefit from AI adoption rather than being displac

analysis:
- **External commentary**: Analysts’ mean price target of $13.21 implies ~25% upside, with a narrow standard deviation of $1.12 indicating relative consensus [1]. A separate narrative-based valuation pegs fair value at $13.81, suggesting ~23.6% undervaluation [2]. A bullish thesis argues the market misprices PATH as a legacy RPA firm threatened by generative AI, whereas the company’s Maestro platform positions it as critical orchestration infrastructure for agentic AI, with governance and compliance needs that LLMs alone cannot fulfill [3]. Unknown: whether these narratives are already discounted by the 23.9% short interest.

- **Why it can work**: The stock trades near 52-week lows (only 14.3% above low) with a forward P/E of 12.3 and PEG of 0.4, suggesting low growth expectations. RSI acceleration from -4.6 to +5.5 (delta +10.1) indicates recent momentum shift. The bullish thesis [3] offers a differentiated catalyst: if enterprises adopt agentic AI, PATH’s orchestration layer could become essential, reversing the obsolescence narrative. Short interest at 23.9% could fuel a squeeze if positive catalysts emerge.

- **What can break it**: The stock has underperformed its sector (1-month return +2.4% vs sector median +4.4%) and remains 47% below 52-week highs. The “value score” of 48.0 is neutral, not compelling. The sentiment score of 0.3 is low. If the agentic AI thesis fails to materialize or competitors (e.g., Microsoft, ServiceNow) offer similar orchestration, PATH could remain a laggard. The 23.9% short interest also reflects persistent skepticism; a short squeeze requires a catalyst, not just low valuation.

- **Confirming evidence to watch**: Look for earnings beats or raised guidance (analyst revisions are positive per [1]). Monitor adoption of Maestro platform in enterprise deals. Watch for short interest reduction (current 23.9% is elevated). A sustained RSI above 50 and price breakout above $12 (lowest analyst target) would signal momentum shift. Unknown: specific product revenue breakdown or customer win data from supplied sources.

- **Bottom line**: PATH presents a contrarian long setup based on a narrative shift from legacy RPA to agentic AI orchestration, supported by low valuation multiples and analyst upside targets. However, the stock is a sector laggard with high short interest and neutral value scores. The thesis is unproven and depends on enterprise AI adoption trends not yet reflected in financials. No trade recommendation.

sources.title: Why Is MGIC (MTG) Down 3.7% Since Last Earnings Report?
sources.excerpt: It has been about a month since the last earnings report for MGIC Investment (MTG). Shares have lost about 3.7% in that time frame, underperforming the S&P 500. But investors have to be wondering, will the recent negative trend continue leading up to its next earnings release, or is MGIC due for a breakout? Before we dive into how investors and analysts have reacted as of late, let's take a quick look at its latest earnings report in order to get a better handle on the important drivers. **MGIC Q1 Earnings Beat, Revenues Miss Estimates, Premiums Down Y/Y** MGIC Investment Corporation reported first-quarter 2026 operating net income per share of 76 cents, which beat the Zacks Consensus Estimate by 4.1%. The bottom line also improved 1.3% year over year. Total operating revenues declined 3% year over year to $297 million, attributable to lower net premiums earned and other revenues. The top line missed the Zacks Consensus Estimate by 1.4%. The quarterly results reflected stable investment income, partially offset by lower net premiums earned and other revenues. ### Operational Update Insurance in force increased 3% year over year to $302.7 billion, exceeding the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $293.7 billion as well as our estimate of $295.6 billion. Meanwhile, primary delinquency rose 6.2% to 27,006 loans during the quarter. Net premiums earned declined 3.4% year over year to $235.4 million, surpassing our estimate of $234.3 million. Meanwhile, net investment income increased 0.5% year over year to $61.7 million, but came in below our estimate of $61.8 million and the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $62.4 million. Persistency, the percentage of insurance remaining in force, was 84% as of March 31, 2026, and declined 70 basis points from the year-ago quarter's level. Meanwhile, n

sources.title: Is Softer Q1 Results With Steady EPS Altering The Investment Case For MGIC Investment (MTG)?
sources.excerpt: * In late April 2026, MGIC Investment Corporation reported first-quarter revenue of US$297.08 million and net income of US$165.3 million, both lower than a year earlier, while diluted earnings per share from continuing operations held at US$0.76. * The combination of softer top- and bottom-line results alongside management's emphasis on rising new insurance written and disciplined capital return presents a mixed picture of MGIC's operating momentum. * Next, we'll examine how softer revenue and net income alongside resilient per-share earnings could influence MGIC Investment's existing 2026 investment narrative. Uncover the next big thing with [25 elite penny stocks](https://simplywall.st/discover/investing-ideas/472945/elite-penny- stocks/us?utm_medium=finance_user&utm_campaign=intro&utm_source=yahoo&blueprint=4570155) that balance risk and reward. ## MGIC Investment Investment Narrative Recap To own MGIC Investment, you need to believe that a steady, if mature, mortgage insurance franchise can keep turning a large, high quality book into consistent per share earnings and capital returns. The latest quarter's softer revenue and net income, alongside essentially flat diluted EPS, does not appear to materially change the near term focus on disciplined buybacks as a key catalyst or the existing risk that limited policy growth could cap longer term earnings power. Against this backdrop, the board's fresh authorization of up to US$750,000,000 in share repurchases through the end of 2028 is highly relevant, because it reinforces how much of MGIC's 2026 story rests on capital return rather than organic expansion. For investors weighing this, the combination of modest top line slippage and a renewed commitment to reducing the share count sharpens the trade off between supportin

analysis:
- **External commentary**: [1] notes analyst price targets are static with no revisions, implying a lack of fresh bullish catalysts from the sell-side. [2] reports Q1 earnings beat EPS estimates by 4.1% but revenues missed by 1.4%, with net premiums earned down 3.4% year-over-year and primary delinquency up 6.2%. [3] highlights a mixed picture: softer revenue and net income, flat diluted EPS, and a new $750M buyback authorization through 2028, emphasizing capital return over organic growth.

- **Why it can work**: The stock trades at a forward P/E of 7.64 and a P/B of 1.07, with a PEG ratio of 0.4, suggesting deep value relative to earnings growth. The $750M buyback authorization [3] could support per-share earnings and provide a floor, especially if the company executes aggressively. Low short interest (3.8% of float) and a composite value score of 72.2 indicate the market may not fully price in capital return potential.

- **What can break it**: Revenue is declining (down 3% year-over-year) [2], and net premiums earned continue to shrink, signaling limited organic growth. Primary delinquency rose 6.2% [2], which could pressure loss reserves if the housing market weakens. Persistency fell 70 basis points [2], suggesting policy retention challenges. Without analyst upgrades [1] or revenue acceleration, the stock may remain a value trap.

- **Confirming evidence to watch**: Track quarterly net premiums earned and new insurance written trends for stabilization or growth. Monitor buyback execution pace relative to the $750M authorization. Watch delinquency rates and persistency—any deterioration would weaken the thesis. A catalyst would be analyst target revisions upward [1], which are currently absent.

- **Bottom line**: MTG offers a cheap valuation and a large buyback program, but the qualitative data shows revenue contraction, rising delinquencies, and no analyst enthusiasm. The long setup depends on capital return offsetting weak fundamentals, which is uncertain without evidence of organic improvement. No trade recommendation.

sources.title: Jim Cramer gives Elon Musk's SpaceX a ringing endorsement after Friday's historic debut - CNBC
sources.excerpt: Years ago, when Tesla went public, I said, "sell, sell, sell," because I could not see a path to profitability. It was June 2010, and Tesla was a car company — plain and simple. While exciting — I know, because I test drove one early — I was not able to get my head around it. Nine years later, I felt Tesla was morphing into so much more, and I went positive. We caught 96% of the move up. It was one of my best calls. However, Elon Musk never forgave me and endorsed the "tiresome" contraindicator slapstick that has dogged me for years. Even two years ago, I heard that he didn't want to say hello to me because of that "sell" call. In some ways, I wish I had been willing to be wrong or to change my tune to something very positive. I am true to myself, though, which often makes for misery. I would have liked the dialogue my colleagues have with Musk. Yes, I am jealous and petty, good qualities of a competitive journalist. No, I couldn't go positive, because I have had the same mission from when I started TheStreet.com in 1995. Total transparency. I was afraid of being wrong, of course. I wasn't. No matter. It's too late. It was sure good for readers and watchers, though. That's really all that matters. I preface this weekend's column with that story because I wanted you to know that this time around, I am all in. This time around, there is so much more to SpaceX than just a dish company or a satellite launcher, or even an X, which was part of the xAI operation that Musk bought in February. I view SpaceX as a company with some of the greatest optionality of any newly public entity. Every single division is on the come. I love my Starlink, and it costs $100 less, and it is much faster than the cable and wireless company that preceded it. I think that it could be the word proce

analysis:
- **External commentary**: Rosenblatt lowered its price target on CMCSA to $24 from $30, maintaining a Neutral rating, citing a tougher broadband market. Key headwinds identified include post-pandemic declines in moving activity (limiting new subscriber opportunities), the success of fixed wireless offerings from T-Mobile and other wireless carriers, and growth from fiber over-builders [1]. Separately, the stock was listed as one of the “Most Undervalued Long Term Stocks to Buy Right Now” [1]. No bullish analyst upgrade or positive catalyst from the excerpts is present.

- **Why it can work**: The stock trades at a forward P/E of 6.28 and price-to-book of 0.96, suggesting deep value relative to historical norms. The “value_laggard_score” of 79.1 and “composite_value_score” of 72.0 indicate the stock is cheap versus peers. If broadband subscriber losses stabilize or fixed wireless competition peaks, the low valuation could attract mean-reversion or activist interest. The RSI acceleration from -2.1 to +5.0 suggests short-term momentum may be turning.

- **What can break it**: The primary risk is structural erosion in the core broadband business, as detailed by Rosenblatt [1]. Fixed wireless and fiber over-builders are taking market share, and the post-pandemic moving slowdown limits organic growth. The stock is 35.5% below its 52-week high and underperformed its sector over the past month (-4.4% vs. -2.5% median). The PEG ratio of 142.98 implies negligible expected earnings growth, making the low P/E less compelling. No source provides evidence of a near-term catalyst.

- **Confirming evidence to watch**: Look for stabilization or improvement in broadband subscriber net additions in the next quarterly report. Monitor T-Mobile and fiber builder market share data for signs of deceleration. Watch for insider buying or a significant increase in short interest (currently low at 2.3%) as a contrarian signal. Any analyst upgrade or positive revision to forward estimates would be a key confirming event. Unknown from sources: management’s specific plans to address broadband competition or capital allocation strategy.

- **Bottom line**: CMCSA is a cheap, lagging telecom/ media stock facing well-documented structural headwinds in broadband. The valuation is low, but the low PEG and negative price momentum suggest the market is pricing in continued earnings pressure. Without a catalyst for revenue or subscriber growth reversal, the cheapness alone is not a sufficient long setup. The external commentary provides no bullish thesis—only a neutral analyst note and a generic “undervalued” label. No trade recommendation.

sources.title: What Starwood Property Trust (STWD)'s New US$600 Million 2031 Notes Offering Means For Shareholders
sources.excerpt: * In May 2026, Starwood Property Trust, Inc. completed a private offering of US$600 million of 6.125% senior unsecured notes due June 1, 2031, with proceeds earmarked for redeeming existing debt, funding general corporate purposes, and supporting eligible green and social projects. * The transaction both extends the company's debt maturity profile and signals a growing emphasis on financing assets with environmental and social benefits, which can matter for income-focused and sustainability-oriented investors alike. * We'll now examine how this new US$600 million unsecured note issuance, and its green and social allocation plans, may influence Starwood's investment narrative. AI is about to change healthcare. These [40 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery](https://simplywall.st/discover/investing-ideas/434426/healthcare- ai/us?utm_medium=finance_user&utm_campaign=intro&utm_source=yahoo&blueprint=4611485). The best part - they are all under $10b in market cap - there's still time to get in early. ## Starwood Property Trust Investment Narrative Recap To own Starwood Property Trust, you need to be comfortable with a mortgage REIT that leans on diversified real estate credit, consistent dividends, and active use of debt markets. The new US$600,000,000 6.125% senior notes modestly ease near term refinancing needs but do not remove key risks around nonaccrual assets, sector stress in parts of the portfolio, or the reliance on continued market access to support funding and dividend coverage. Among recent developments, UBS's renewed Buy rating and US$20–21 price target for Starwood sits alongside this latest bond issue, highlighting how closely the equity story is tied to funding costs, balance sheet flexibility, and expectations for returns

analysis:
- **External commentary**: Sources note STWD’s stock declined 7.4% YTD and 6.9% over the past year, closing at ~$17.05, with recent momentum fading versus longer-term returns [1]. A $600M 6.125% senior notes offering (due 2031) extends debt maturity and signals a green/social financing shift, but does not remove key risks around nonaccrual assets or sector stress [2]. UBS maintains a Buy rating with a $20–21 price target, linking equity value to funding costs and balance sheet flexibility [2].

- **Why it can work**: The stock trades at a forward P/E of 8.81 and price-to-book of 0.92, suggesting discounted valuation relative to historical or peer levels. The new notes offering reduces near-term refinancing risk by extending maturities, and the green/social allocation may attract sustainability-focused capital [2]. A 3-year total shareholder return of 19.11% indicates underlying asset performance, despite recent price drift [1].

- **What can break it**: The RSI acceleration score is -16.6, indicating sharp negative momentum, and the stock is only 1.2% above its 52-week low. The 1-month return of -0.5% lags the sector median of +4.0% and peer lag of +4.5%, suggesting persistent underperformance. Nonaccrual assets and sector stress in parts of the portfolio are cited as unresolved risks [2]. The high 6.125% coupon on new debt increases interest expense, potentially pressuring dividend coverage.

- **Confirming evidence to watch**: Monitor upcoming quarterly earnings for changes in nonaccrual loan levels and net interest margin. Track dividend payout ratio relative to funds from operations (FFO) to assess coverage sustainability. Watch for any further insider buying or analyst upgrades beyond UBS. A sustained move above the 50-day moving average or a positive RSI crossover would signal momentum reversal.

- **Bottom line**: STWD presents a contrarian long setup based on cheap valuation (P/B < 1, forward P/E ~9) and improved debt maturity profile, but near-term price action is weak and sector headwinds remain. The opportunity score of 74.8 reflects value laggard status, yet the RSI acceleration score of 7.2 and negative momentum suggest no clear catalyst for re-rating. No trade recommendation.
